skill.name: translate
skill.description: Translates Markdown and plain text between languages with glossary creation, locale-aware style guidance, batch processing, and helper scripts for large Markdown files. The default target language is Chinese.
skill.summary: Translate Markdown and plain text with consistent terminology and locale-aware style.
skill.author: FuHehe12

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but the reviewed evidence shows most are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code, examples, and the .md file context. No malware intent, network exfiltration, environment access, or prompt-injection text was found; the confirmed risk is legitimate local filesystem work through Bash commands and Python helper scripts.

medium_findings.title: Bash Workflow Can Modify Local Files
medium_findings.description: The skill enables Bash and instructs agents to run local Python utilities and cleanup commands. This is expected for Markdown splitting and merging, but incorrect paths or working directories could alter or remove unintended local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in the workflow and include local cleanup with rm -rf. The risk is operational rather than malicious because paths are intended to target generated translation artifacts.

medium_findings.title: Helper Scripts Write To User-Specified Paths
medium_findings.description: The split and merge utilities accept input, output directory, manifest, and output file paths, then read or write files through pathlib. This is necessary for the skill, but it creates filesystem impact if a user supplies broad or sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly create directories and write Markdown, context JSON, manifests, and merged output files. No shell injection or network behavior is present, so the risk is bounded to local file writes.

medium_findings.title: Sub-Agent Translation Prompts Process Untrusted Text
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends source segments, context files, samples, and tone guidance to sub-agents for translation. No prompt-injection text was found in the skill files, but user-supplied documents could contain instructions that conflict with translation boundaries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sub-agent delegation is documented and intentional. The concern depends on future user-provided source content, so confidence is lower than for direct filesystem behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick And Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution matches are Markdown fences or inline-code markers, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts appear to be triggered by Markdown text, examples, file extensions, or ordinary control-flow code, not cryptographic use or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations show Markdown syntax, documentation, or regex checks for fenced code blocks. No evidence was found for cryptographic APIs, Ruby execution, or reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Sample File Is Human-Readable Text
low_findings.description: The entropy alert on the sample file is not supported by semantic review. The file contains readable translation examples and style notes, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire file was readable Markdown with source text, translations, and explanations. No encoded payload, binary blob, or loader pattern was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Cleanup Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow documents rm -rf for removing the generated _parts directory. This is common cleanup behavior, but it should only run from the intended source directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive command is directly documented. It targets a relative generated directory, which reduces but does not remove operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path-Based File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Helper scripts write generated parts, context files, manifests, and merged output to paths derived from command-line arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write_text calls and output path handling are explicit. The pattern is legitimate for this skill, but it is still a meaningful filesystem capability.

content.value_statement: Long translations often lose terminology, formatting, and tone across files. This skill creates a glossary, style brief, and batch workflow to keep Markdown translations consistent.
actual_capabilities: Translates Markdown and plain text between language pairs, with Chinese as the default target language.
Creates temporary glossary and translation brief files before translation starts.
Uses locale guidance for zh-CN, zh-TW, zh-HK, zh-SG, zh-MY, and English variants.
Splits large Markdown files into semantic parts and merges translated parts afterward.
Preserves Markdown structure, links, YAML frontmatter, and code blocks during translation.
Supports parallel sub-agent translation for large files and multi-file batches.
limitations: Non-Markdown formats must be converted to Markdown before use.
Large-file workflows create temporary files and require careful path handling.
The skill does not provide certified legal, medical, or official translation review.
Quality depends on the source text, glossary quality, and user-provided style samples.

use_cases.title: Translate technical documentation
use_cases.description: Convert developer guides or README files while preserving Markdown, terminology, and code block boundaries.

use_cases.title: Localize product content
use_cases.description: Prepare product copy or tutorials for a specific Chinese-speaking locale with consistent vocabulary and tone.

use_cases.title: Process long Markdown manuscripts
use_cases.description: Split, translate, verify, and merge long documents through a controlled batch workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Translate a short document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one Markdown or text file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this Markdown file into Chinese. Preserve headings, links, lists, tables, YAML frontmatter, and code blocks.

prompt_templates.title: Translate for a locale
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs region-specific vocabulary and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this document into zh-TW. Use Taiwan terminology and preserve the author's register. Add term notes only when needed.

prompt_templates.title: Use a project glossary
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate these Markdown files into zh-CN. Build a shared glossary first, keep product terms consistent, and do not overwrite source files.

prompt_templates.title: Run a large batch workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for long files or multiple files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this large Markdown project into Chinese. Create a translation brief, split large files, process parts in parallel, verify boundaries, then merge the output.

output_examples.input: A short technical paragraph about model deployment and inference latency.
output_examples.output: A concise Chinese translation that keeps terms like model, latency, and inference consistent with the glossary.

output_examples.input: A Markdown README with headings, links, lists, and command examples.
output_examples.output: A translated Markdown document with the same structure, translated link text, unchanged URLs, and untouched code blocks.

output_examples.input: A long multi-section guide that exceeds the direct translation threshold.
output_examples.output: Split translated sections, a merged final document, and a short report about formatting and terminology checks.

best_practices: Specify the target language, locale, audience, and desired tone before translation begins.
Review the generated glossary and brief for important projects before processing many files.
Keep source files backed up and confirm paths before running split, merge, or cleanup commands.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to translate PDFs, images, or Word documents without converting them to Markdown first.
Do not skip glossary creation for terminology-heavy or multi-file projects.
Do not run cleanup commands from an uncertain working directory.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports any language pair that the underlying model can understand. Chinese is the default target language.

faq.question: Can it translate Markdown files without breaking formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow preserves headings, links, tables, YAML frontmatter, and code blocks.

faq.question: Does it overwrite the original source file?
faq.answer: No. The workflow writes translated output to a separate file with a language suffix.

faq.question: How does it handle long documents?
faq.answer: It can split large Markdown files into parts, translate them in parallel, and merge the results.

faq.question: Can I control terminology?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill creates a temporary glossary and can use a persistent user glossary for repeated terms.

faq.question: Is this suitable for certified translation?
faq.answer: No. It is a writing and localization aid, not a certified translation service.
